content.problemStatement:
In C, once you declare an array, you can also initialize it by assigning values to it right away.
Initialization means giving the array its starting set of values either all at once or one element at a time.\
Array indexes start at `0`, so the first element is at position `0`.

**Syntax:**

```c
data_type array_name[size] = {value1, value2, value3, ...};
```

---

**Full Initialization with Specified Size:**

```c
int numbers[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50};  // Creates an integer array of size 5 with predefined values
```

**Initialization Without Specifying Size:**

```c
float temperatures[] = {98.6, 99.2, 97.8};  // Compiler automatically sets size to 3 based on the number of values
```

**Partial Initialization (Remaining Elements Set to 0 or '\0'):**

```c
char vowels[5] = {'a', 'e', 'i'};   // Remaining elements (index 3 and 4) will be '\0' (null characters)
```

**All Elements Initialized to 0:**

```c
int scores[10] = {0};  // All 10 elements are initialized to 0
```
